" <<The information specialist>> <<uses graph theory>> <<to model journals>> <<as a weighted>> <<directed graph>> <<G equals>>. <<Each vertex represents>> <<a journal or database>> <<Each edge represents>> <<a citation or reference>> <<with frequency as>> <<its weight. The adjacency>> <<matrix A captures>> <<these relationships>> <<with Aij being>> <<the weight from>> <<vertex i to j.>>

* * *

<<The matrix exponential>> <<e^A is defined>> <<as the sum>> <<of A^k over>> <<k from zero>> <<to infinity. In continuous>> <<time random walks>> <<each step is>> <<weighted by time>> <<and probabilities>> <<are modeled>> <<through exponentials. The element>> <<(e^A)ij counts>> <<expected paths>> <<from i to j>> <<across all possible>> <<walk lengths.>>

* * *

<<To reduce storage>> <<she applies SVD>> <<to matrix A. SVD>> <<decomposes A into>> <<U, Sigma, and V>> <<transposed. The optimal>> <<low rank Bk is>> <<formed by truncating>> <<Sigma to rank k. This>> <<minimizes Frobenius>> <<norm of A minus>> <<Bk by keeping>> <<largest singular>> <<values. The result>> <<is a compressed>> <<version of A.>>"
